claim: MaceOffExtractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor to aggregate BDE-derived effective k_cat estimates from the existing cell_sim.layer1_atomic.engine.MACEBackend across an enzyme's substrate set. Cache parquet in commit ebbfdff is REGISTERED BUT EMPTY (455 rows, mace_has_estimate=0.0 for every row, all other 6 columns NaN) because Syn3A's SBML encodes metabolites as BiGG-style species IDs (M_atp_c, M_h2o_c, ...) not SMILES, and MACE-OFF needs real SMILES to compute BDEs. Notebook cell 7 now carries a HAVE_CURATED_SMILES_MAP=False guard that skips the backend entirely rather than pass species IDs as SMILES (which would produce thousands of RDKit parse errors and a 155-row-mostly-NaN parquet). The empty parquet is deliberately tracked by the manifest so Session 15 can swap in a curated species->SMILES map (KEGG / ChEBI) without touching the FeatureRegistry contract.

value.name: mace_off_kcat
value.cache_content_status: empty by design: no curated SBML species -> SMILES map yet; every row has mace_has_estimate=0.0 and all 6 numeric columns NaN

source_detail: Session 14. Verifiable by reading the module + running 5 sandbox-safe tests. The wrapper does NOT re-implement the BDE / Eyring / Hammond math — that already lives in MACEBackend.estimate_kcat since a prior session. External weights: mace-off23_small from the mace-torch package (Batatia et al. 2023). Those weights are NOT registered as a memory_bank source; the extractor is transparent plumbing around the existing layer1_atomic interface.

context.commit_range: TBD — filled in once Session 14 push lands.

caveats: Cache parquet populated in commit ebbfdff but CONTENT IS EMPTY: 455 rows of NaN + mace_has_estimate=0.0 for every CDS. Reason documented in the claim: Syn3A's SBML uses BiGG species IDs (M_atp_c, ...) not SMILES, and MACE-OFF needs real SMILES.

Notebook cell 7 currently has HAVE_CURATED_SMILES_MAP=False which skips the backend entirely. When Session 15 provides a curated species->SMILES map (KEGG or ChEBI), flipping the flag to True reinvokes MaceOffExtractor.extract and populates the parquet with real BDE-derived k_cat aggregates.

Per-substrate detail is aggregated into per-enzyme summary stats so the output satisfies the FeatureRegistry locus_tag index contract. A future session can publish a separate sidecar source (e.g. 'mace_off_per_substrate') if the joint detector stack needs it.
mace-off23_small weights (~100 MB) are NOT in the repo; they download inside MACEBackend._ensure_loaded on the Colab GPU when the curated SMILES map is available.
No MCC measurement is claimed by this fact.
